name: html-to-text
description: Strip HTML tags + script/style/comment blocks, returning visible text. Built for the fetch → extract → LLM pattern (competitive watch, content monitoring) where raw HTML is too noisy and too fuel-expensive for the LLM to process directly. Caps input at MAX_INPUT_BYTES (default 60_000) and output at MAX_VISIBLE_CHARS (default 8_000) so a 1MB marketing page still fits in a sane fuel budget. Reads upstream `body` from either the http-request module's accumulated output or a passed-in `body` field.

MAX_INPUT_BYTES.description: Hard cap on bytes scanned from the upstream HTML body. Default 60000. Most pricing/positioning content is in the first ~50KB of well-built marketing pages; capping below the full page size is the single biggest WASM-fuel lever.

MAX_VISIBLE_CHARS.description: Cap on output visible characters after tag stripping. Default 8000 (~2000 LLM tokens). The scanner stops walking input once this cap is reached.

INPUT_FIELD.description: Override the upstream field path to read HTML from. Default tries (in order): __accumulated__.<source-node-id>, __accumulated__.fetch, body, input.body. Use when your upstream node uses a non-standard field name.
